U.S. Auto Lending | Executive Story
Scale Originations Mix Losses Negative equity Gen Z EV AI Implications
Capital One Auto Lending Strategy | Public-source executive story

Auto lending is still huge.
Growth is not.

A scroll narrative built around public charts only. Minimal text. Exact source URLs included under every visual.

$1.7T outstanding auto debt
23.4M YTD originations
Banks up; captives down
Risk still concentrated by channel
1 | Market context

Scale remains. Easy growth is over.

  • Auto credit is still a $1.7T market.
  • Balance growth slowed to low-single digits.
  • The fight shifts from volume to mix, price, and risk selection.

Household debt composition: auto is one of the largest credit categories

Exact source URL
https://www.newyorkfed.org/medialibrary/interactives/householdcredit/data/pdf/HHDC_2025Q3
NY Fed HHDC 2025Q3, page 3

Outstanding auto debt: $1.7T, but only +0.9% YoY

Exact source URL
https://assets.equifax.com/marketing/US/assets/auto-insights-report-march-2026.pdf
Equifax Automotive Insights Report, March 2026, page 4
2 | Volume and lender mix

2025 recovered, but the engine moved from captives to banks.

  • Origination volumes improved modestly, not dramatically.
  • Banks led the rebound; captives moved backward.
  • The story is lender rotation, not broad demand acceleration.

Highlights of originations: 23.4M YTD, $709.2B original loan amount

Exact source URL
https://assets.equifax.com/marketing/US/assets/auto-insights-report-march-2026.pdf
Equifax Automotive Insights Report, March 2026, page 5

Originations by credit score: the market is still dominated by higher-score borrowers

Exact source URL
https://www.newyorkfed.org/medialibrary/interactives/householdcredit/data/pdf/HHDC_2025Q3
NY Fed HHDC 2025Q3, page 8
3 | Credit mix

Banks re-entered subprime, but channel still matters more than the label.

  • Banks have meaningful subprime exposure again.
  • Dealer finance and monoline remain structurally riskier channels.
  • Vehicle quality and borrower profile still differ sharply by lender type.

CFPB: subprime market share by lender type

Exact source URL
https://files.consumerfinance.gov/f/documents/cfpb_subprime-auto_data-point_2021-09.pdf
CFPB Subprime Auto Loan Outcomes by Lender Type, page 14

CFPB: subprime borrower and vehicle characteristics vary by lender type

Exact source URL
https://files.consumerfinance.gov/f/documents/cfpb_subprime-auto_data-point_2021-09.pdf
CFPB Subprime Auto Loan Outcomes by Lender Type, page 15
4 | Credit risk

Loss pressure is rising, but it is not evenly distributed.

  • Overall delinquency rose.
  • Dealer finance and monoline are far above banks and credit unions.
  • Vintage stress still concentrates in lower-score cohorts.

Current delinquency snapshot by lender type and score band

Exact source URL
https://assets.equifax.com/marketing/US/assets/auto-insights-report-march-2026.pdf
Equifax Automotive Insights Report, March 2026, page 7

Early delinquency vintages: deep subprime still separates quickly

Exact source URL
https://assets.equifax.com/marketing/US/assets/auto-insights-report-march-2026.pdf
Equifax Automotive Insights Report, March 2026, page 8
5 | Used vs. new

Used-vs-new matters because negative equity can hide borrower stress.

  • Negative-equity exposure spiked around 2020 and then eased.
  • New-vehicle deals saw the sharper swing.
  • Captives carried the highest average negative-equity dollars in the CFPB sample.

Share of loans with rolled negative equity over time

Exact source URL
https://files.consumerfinance.gov/f/documents/cfpb_negative-equity-in-auto-lending-report_2024-06.pdf
CFPB Negative Equity in Auto Lending, page 18

Average negative equity by lender type

Exact source URL
https://files.consumerfinance.gov/f/documents/cfpb_negative-equity-in-auto-lending-report_2024-06.pdf
CFPB Negative Equity in Auto Lending, page 20
6 | Emerging borrowers

Gen Z is entering the market differently.

  • Credit unions over-index with Gen Z.
  • Monoline also over-indexes relative to older cohorts.
  • The winning journey is digital, transparent, and low-friction.
Digital-expectation proxy: Deloitte digital banking article — https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/insights/industry/financial-services/gen-z-millennial-digital-banking.html

Originations by generation and lender type

Exact source URL
https://assets.equifax.com/marketing/US/assets/auto-insights-report-march-2026.pdf
Equifax Automotive Insights Report, March 2026, page 6
7 | EV financing

EV is still a mix and residual-value question — not yet a mass-volume one.

  • U.S. consumers still prefer ICE for the next vehicle.
  • Hybrids outperform BEVs in stated next-purchase interest.
  • Financing strategy should focus on ticket size, collateral behavior, and residual risk.

2026 Global Automotive Consumer Study: U.S. next-vehicle preferences

Exact source URL
https://www.deloitte.com/content/dam/assets-zone3/us/en/docs/industries/consumer/2026/2026-global-automotive-consumer-study-jan2026.pdf
Deloitte 2026 Global Automotive Consumer Study, page 7
8 | AI in auto lending

AI has moved from pilot to operating leverage.

  • The value is not only in underwriting.
  • Speed, pricing, collections, fraud, and servicing all matter.
  • The prize is better decisions at lower unit cost.
Auto-finance extension: https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/automotive-and-assembly/our-insights/agentic-ai-a-new-path-to-value-in-the-auto-finance-industry

McKinsey: advanced analytics and ML across the customer life cycle

Exact source URL
https://www.mckinsey.com/~/media/mckinsey/industries/financial%20services/our%20insights/ai%20powered%20decision%20making%20for%20the%20bank%20of%20the%20future/ai-powered-decision-making-for-the-bank-of-the-future.pdf
McKinsey, AI-powered decision making for the bank of the future, page 3
9 | So what for Capital One

The next win is selective growth — not scale at any cost.

Defend the right dealers

Protect prime and mid-prime relationships; exit weak dealer economics faster.

Price near-prime precisely

Use risk-based pricing and better channel filters, not broad subprime expansion.

Flag negative-equity risk

Treat used/new mix and rolled balance as early loss-severity signals.

Turn AI into throughput

Faster approvals, tighter pricing, smarter collections, lower servicing cost.

In this market, portfolio quality compounds. Indiscriminate growth does not.
